title: Podman rootless exposed port not reachable
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug
**Description**

After recreating the nginx container in a named network space, the exposed port is not reachable any more.
Other containers in the same network can reach the container, so I am sure it is running correctly.
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run -d --replace --name nginx --network web -p 8000:80 nginx:stable

2. curl localhost:8000


**Describe the results you received:**
curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 8000: Connection refused

**Describe the results you expected:**
<!DOCTYPE html>
...
</html>

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I used the Network ID (web), so that multily pods can communicate with each other, but i do not have to exposed port. Work great before yeaster day.

podman network ls
```
NETWORK ID    NAME        VERSION     PLUGINS
xxxxxxxxxxxx  podman      0.4.0       bridge,portmap,firewall,tuning
xxxxxxxxxxxx  web         0.4.0       bridge,portmap,firewall,tuning,dnsname
```
Tried recreating it,
changed in containers.conf to userns = "auto", so I tried with flag --userns keep-id without any difference.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
waiting for ubuntu 22.04.1 to Update, should be latest stable for Ubuntu 20.04

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**
Yes.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Still new to podman.

Thanks for any help
Could not find any solutions to my problem. Guessing that the flag --network bridge solution for Podman v3.2 should be the same as having the new Network ID.

PS: tested it with a new Network ID, still not working, but it still works in the default network.

comments.body: use --userns=host, rootless port forwarding with a custom userns and networks is broken in that version. This bug is fixed in v4.0.

Thx alot,
I thought it was fixed in v3.4 and that --userns=keep-id did the same as --userns=host.
but now it is working again.
